name: Evidently AI
pricing_notes: Open source core (Apache 2.0); Evidently Cloud from $80/mo for team features

auth.notes: Open source: no auth. Evidently Cloud: API key for dashboard/alerts.

performance.notes: Local computation; performance depends on dataset size

agent_readiness.notes: ML model and data monitoring framework. Presets for data drift, data quality, regression/classification performance, LLM quality. Beautiful HTML reports and JSON output. Dashboard for online monitoring. Tests framework for data quality assertions. Works standalone or with Evidently Cloud for persistent dashboards. Best open-source option for ML monitoring in production pipelines.

gotchas: Cloud features require paid subscription — open source is self-hosted only
Large datasets may require sampling for acceptable report generation speed
LLM evaluation metrics limited compared to dedicated LLM eval tools
Drift detection statistical methods require minimum sample sizes
best_when: Production ML pipeline monitoring for data drift and model performance
Regular data quality reports for batch ML pipelines
Model validation in CI/CD with pass/fail test assertions
Comparing training vs production data distributions
avoid_when: LLM evaluation (use DeepEval or RAGAS instead)
Teams needing enterprise dashboards without cloud spend
summary: Evidently AI is an open-source ML monitoring and testing framework. Provides prebuilt presets for data drift, data quality, and model performance monitoring. Generates HTML reports and JSON for programmatic use. Tests framework enables CI/CD quality gates. Best open-source option for batch ML pipeline monitoring. Evidently Cloud adds persistent dashboards and alerting.
